name: Huang Zhong
description: Huang Zhong, a renowned general from the late Eastern Han Dynasty and Shu Han during the Three Kingdoms period, is celebrated for his exceptional archery skills and unwavering loyalty. Initially serving under Liu Biao and later Han Xuan, Huang Zhong was greatly underappreciated until he joined Liu Bei's forces. Despite his advanced age, he demonstrated remarkable prowess in battle, most notably during the conquest of Changsha. He famously fought Guan Yu to a standstill, earning Guan Yu's respect and eventually switching his allegiance to Liu Bei. Huang Zhong's bravery and martial abilities were instrumental in Liu Bei's campaigns, including the capture of Chengdu and the Hanzhong Campaign. He was later appointed as one of the Five Tiger Generals of Shu Han, a testament to his military achievements and unwavering dedication. Huang Zhong's story is a powerful reminder that age is no barrier to greatness and that true talent will always shine through adversity. His skills with the bow and arrow are legendary, and he is often depicted as an elderly warrior with unmatched accuracy and strength. He embodies the virtues of loyalty, courage, and perseverance, making him a beloved figure in Chinese folklore and a symbol of heroic valor.
subtitle: The Old Archer of Unmatched Skill
achievements: Fighting Guan Yu to a draw
Becoming one of the Five Tiger Generals

keyMyths.title: The Duel with Guan Yu
keyMyths.description: During the conquest of Changsha, Huang Zhong was ordered to fight Guan Yu. Despite his age, Huang Zhong fought Guan Yu to a standstill, even sparing Guan Yu's life when his horse stumbled.

keyMyths.title: Killing Xiahou Yuan
keyMyths.description: During the Hanzhong Campaign, Huang Zhong, though elderly, ambushed and killed Xiahou Yuan, a key general of Cao Cao, significantly weakening Cao Cao's forces in the region.

keyMyths.title: Joining Liu Bei
keyMyths.description: Initially serving under Han Xuan, Huang Zhong was almost executed due to false accusations. Impressed by Liu Bei's righteousness, Huang Zhong ultimately chose to join Liu Bei's forces, marking a turning point in his career.

sources.author: Luo Guanzhong
sources.description: A historical novel that romanticizes the events of the Three Kingdoms period, featuring Huang Zhong prominently.
sources.text: A historical novel that romanticizes the events of the Three Kingdoms period, featuring Huang Zhong prominently.

sources.author: Chen Shou
sources.description: A historical text that provides a more factual account of the Three Kingdoms period, with mentions of Huang Zhong's military contributions.
